evaluation.summary: The primorial-adjusted Benford model in Base-210 was statistically rejected: it fits empirical prime leading-digit distributions *worse* than a naive uniform baseline (KL divergence 0.788 vs 0.558), and the conclusions section fraudulently inverts this finding.
next_directions: Audit and fix the conclusions-generation code, which is inverting the actual KL divergence comparison result
Revisit the theoretical derivation of the primorial-adjusted model to understand why it underperforms even a uniform distribution
Test alternative base choices (e.g., Base-30 or Base-2310) to determine whether the failure is specific to Base-210 or structural to the approach
